title: IPL 2026: How every piece of the puzzle fell into place for Royal Challengers Bengaluru
content: Every major RCB win in IPL 2026 featured a different hero. Sometimes it was Kohli. Sometimes Patidar. Sometimes Padikkal. Sometimes David. Sometimes Krunal. Sometimes Bhuvneshwar. Sometimes it was the bowlers operating as a collective unit. That was the defining characteristic of this championship side. The first IPL trophy ended one of the league's longest waits. The second confirmed something even more important. Royal Challengers Bengaluru are no longer a franchise chasing history. They may well be on their way to creating one.

title: Virat Kohli's dhol dance with Pandya goes viral after RCB's IPL 2026 title triumph
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured their second consecutive IPL title by defeating Gujarat Titans. Star batter Virat Kohli and all-rounder Krunal Pandya celebrated the historic win with dhols. Kohli's unbeaten 75 guided RCB to a clinical chase. This victory makes RCB the third franchise to defend their IPL crown. Kohli was named Player of the Match for his exceptional performance.

title: California University faculty sign open letter seeking return of standardised math, science test
content: University of California faculty are urging the reinstatement of SAT/ACT math requirements for STEM applicants, citing a significant decline in student math skills since the tests were dropped. They argue current admissions practices fail to ensure readiness, forcing instructors to reteach basic math and jeopardizing the quality of STEM education.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi creates IPL history, becomes first player to win...
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi etched his name in IPL history, becoming the first player to clinch five major individual awards in the 2026 season. Despite Rajasthan Royals missing the final, Sooryavanshi dominated, leading in runs with 776 and smashing a record 72 sixes. His stellar performance earned him the Orange Cap, MVP, Emerging Player, Super Striker, and Super Sixes awards.

title: Veteran singer Suman Kalyanpur passes away at 89, fans pay tribute
content: Veteran playback singer Suman Kalyanpur, known for her voice similar to Lata Mangeshkar, passed away at 89. She enchanted listeners for decades with her versatile repertoire in Hindi, Marathi, and other regional languages. Her demise marks the end of a golden era in Indian music.

title: 'Amazon would collapse if run like New York City': Jeff Bezos takes swipe at Mamdani
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has endorsed New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new government efficiency initiative, COGE. This comes after their public disagreement on taxing the wealthy. Bezos, who previously criticized city spending, now supports Mamdani's plan to make government work smarter, suggesting savings could benefit lower earners.

title: Iran president Pezeshkian submits resignation letter over IRGC control: Report
content: Reports emerged of Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian submitting a resignation letter, citing exclusion from decision-making and IRGC control. He reportedly stated an inability to govern effectively under these circumstances. However, an official from the president's office dismissed these claims as baseless rumors and wishful thinking from foreign networks.

title: JP Morgan CEO vows to fight against Act disputed by banking and crypto industries
content: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has fiercely criticized Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong and vowed to oppose the Clarity Act, a digital-asset bill advancing in Congress. Dimon dismissed Armstrong's stance and stated banks will not accept the current legislation, which centers on stablecoin interest payments. Banks argue these yields lack regulatory safeguards, while crypto firms see them as customer rewards.

title: 'Drishyam 3' BO day 11: Mohanlal film sees growth again
content: Mohanlal's 'Drishyam 3' is dominating the box office, crossing Rs 96.70 crore in India and Rs 116.75 crore overseas. The mystery drama continues its strong run into its second weekend, with Kerala leading regional collections.  The film's worldwide total now stands at an impressive Rs 228.95 crore, captivating audiences.

title: Gujarat Titans players evacuated after team bus suffers short circuit; all safe
content: A disappointing IPL final defeat was followed by a tense off-field scare for Gujarat Titans as a short circuit in the team bus left players stranded for nearly an hour. The incident capped a gruelling week of travel, weather disruptions and high-pressure knockout matches. GT lost the final by five wickets against RCB at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: Packers, Lions emerge for Colts’ $33m QB landing spot
content: The Indianapolis Colts’ star player Anthony Richardson is actively seeking a trade since the last few weeks even as he continues to attend the team’s OTAs, sparking confusion among all the fans. While a number of teams have been linked to him, it seems the Green Bay Packers and the Detroit Lions have emerged as strong landing spots for the star player.

title: Declan Rice upset over attacks on girlfriend Lauren Fryer before World Cup
content: Arsenal star midfielder Declan Rice has been eyeing the 2026 World Cup but his relationship with his girlfriend, Lauren Fryer, has continued to dominate the headlines, even as their relationship gets brutally mocked. While Declan Rice has often praised his girlfriend and the mother of his child, Lauren Fryer, for her support, he has often been mocked for her appearance. Amid this, Declan Rice’s comments about how he feels about it have sparked chaos.

title: Blake Lively reportedly hits breaking point amid Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce fallout
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have remained distant and private about their feelings for the Hollywood power couple, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, as they gear up to get married in the next few weeks in New York City. As speculations swirl about whether Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds will be attending Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s high profile wedding, it seems the actress has reached a breaking point in her life.

title: IPL 2026 prize money: How much did champions RCB and runners-up GT earn this season?
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured the IPL 2026 title and a ₹20 crore prize. Gujarat Titans finished as runners-up, earning ₹13 crore. Rajasthan Royals received ₹7 crore for their third-place finish. Sunrisers Hyderabad took home ₹6.5 crore. The total prize pool for the four playoff teams was over ₹46.5 crore, concluding a successful season.

title: 'Rises again as champions! Karnataka CM-elect DKS, Sidda congratulate RCB on winning IPL
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru has won its second consecutive Indian Premier League title. Chief Minister-designate DK Shivakumar and outgoing Chief Minister Siddaramaiah congratulated the team. They praised the players for their performance and dedication. Celebrations are underway across Bengaluru and Karnataka. Police have issued guidelines for public safety during the festivities.

title: RCB's new era, Kohli's old magic: Defending champions outclass GT in IPL final
content: Virat Kohli produced a masterful unbeaten 75 after Royal Challengers Bengaluru's (RCB) pace attack dismantled Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Sunday. Rajat Patidar's men secured a second successive IPL title with a commanding five-wicket victory. Rajat Patidar also became only the third captain to have won back-to-back IPL titles after MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma.

title: Acquittal doesn’t guarantee back wages after conviction-based dismissal: HC
content: The Chhattisgarh High Court ruled against a former power distribution employee seeking full back wages after acquittal in a graft case. The court stated that a later acquittal does not automatically nullify the legal impact of a valid dismissal order issued during a conviction. The employee was dismissed following a conviction but later acquitted on appeal.

title: Watch: Virat Kohli seals IPL title with six, Anushka Sharma erupts in wild celebration
content: Virat Kohli's masterful unbeaten 75 guided Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a thrilling five-wicket victory over Gujarat Titans in the IPL 2026 final. Kohli struck the winning six, securing RCB's second IPL title and igniting jubilant celebrations. His rapid half-century and calm anchoring of the chase proved decisive in the dramatic finale.

title: IPL final: How Bhuvi, Hazlewood and Rasikh blew GT away with a bowling masterclass
content: RCB's pace trio of Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Josh Hazlewood and Rasikh Salam Dar exploited hard lengths to perfection, sharing seven wickets and dismantling the Gujarat Titans' batting line-up before restricting them to 155 for 8. For GT, Washington Sundar's 50 not out was the top score.

title: More than 45 killed, around 70 injured in blast at explosives storage building in Myanmar
content: An explosion at a building reportedly storing mining explosives in northeastern Myanmar killed over 45 people and injured around 70. The blast occurred Sunday in Kaungtup village, near the Chinese border, an area controlled by the TNLA. Rescue efforts are ongoing, with 46 bodies recovered and 74 injured taken to the hospital.

title: ‘Stuff you dream of... wanting to hit the winning run’: Kohli reveals lifelong IPL wish
content: Chasemaster Virat Kohli fulfilled a long-held dream by hitting the winning six to secure Royal Challengers Bengaluru's second IPL title. His unbeaten 75, featuring his fastest IPL fifty, guided RCB to a five-wicket victory. Kohli emphasised the team's strength and the fans' unwavering support as key to their championship success.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for June 1
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for June 1, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: ‘I feel a bit under pressure’: Sooryavanshi’s hilarious confession steals show - Watch
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, despite Rajasthan Royals not reaching the IPL 2026 final, emerged as a standout performer. He clinched the Orange Cap with a record-breaking 776 runs at a staggering strike rate, alongside the MVP and Emerging Player awards. His humble admission of nervousness during the presentation charmed audiences, highlighting the young talent's remarkable season.

title: Watch: Anushka gives Virat a standing ovation after his match-defining fifty
content: Virat Kohli delivered a match-winning performance in the IPL 2026 final, scoring his fastest-ever IPL half-century off 25 balls. His wife, Anushka Sharma, was seen giving him a standing ovation from the stands, celebrating his crucial knock that helped Royal Challengers Bengaluru secure back-to-back titles.

title: As ACs run nonstop in 45°C heat, are Delhi homes facing a growing fire hazard?
content: Delhi-NCR's sweltering heat is pushing ACs to their limits, sparking a rise in dangerous fires. Faulty wiring, overloaded circuits, and poor maintenance are increasingly causing AC-related blazes, with recent fatal incidents highlighting the urgent need for awareness. Experts warn that ignoring early warning signs like burning smells or flickering lights can have devastating consequences.

title: Virat Kohli scripts history, smashes fastest IPL fifty of his career in final
content: Virat Kohli delivered a masterclass in the IPL 2026 final, smashing the fastest half-century of his career in just 25 balls. Despite a mid-innings collapse, Kohli's composed innings guided Royal Challengers Bengaluru in their chase of 156 against Gujarat Titans. His remarkable performance placed him among the fastest fifties in IPL final history.

title: Watch: Monkey snatches Rs 2 lakh cash bag at UP court, throws notes from tree
content: A monkey caused chaos at a Uttar Pradesh court complex by snatching a bag containing ₹2 lakh in cash. The primate climbed a tree and began tearing open the bag, scattering ₹500 notes. Authorities later recovered ₹1.98 lakh from the scene.

title: TMC defers Mamata-led MLAs' meeting after majority skip amid Abhishek, Kalyan Banerjee attack row
content: A Trinamool Congress (TMC) meeting was postponed after nearly three-fourths of the party's 80 MLAs failed to attend, following alleged attacks on Abhishek Banerjee and Kalyan Banerjee. The party announced statewide rallies and a sit-in in Kolkata against post-poll violence and evictions, blaming BJP for "terror" against workers. BJP denies involvement, citing local anger.

title: Bhuvneshwar scripts history, equals record for most wickets by Indian bowler in...
content: Bhuvneshwar Kumar equalled Mohammed Shami's record of 17 powerplay wickets in a single IPL season during the IPL 2026 final. His crucial dismissal of Sai Sudharsan helped RCB put Gujarat Titans under pressure early in the match. This achievement further solidifies Bhuvneshwar's impact this season and places him in contention for the Purple Cap.

title: Two rupees for cancer: Inside India's smokeless tobacco epidemic that no one is stopping
content: Smokeless tobacco, a cheap and accessible addiction, traps millions of Indians in poverty. Despite graphic warnings, products like gutka remain under-regulated, costing mere rupees. This article highlights the urgent need for stricter policies on affordability, availability, and appeal to combat this pervasive public health crisis.

title: West Bengal Cabinet expansion: 35 more Ministers to be sworn in on June 1
content: With this expansion on June 1, Cabinet strength will rise to 41; along with seniority, the BJP is likely to give considerations to regional and community-wise representations

title: 15-day ultimatum to Rabri Devi to vacate Patna bungalow
content: RJD leaders say Minister Ram should question why government is repeatedly changing his residence, after he claimed Rabri Devi was refusing to vacate because he was from the Dalit community

title: Rahul targets Centre over CBSE OSM row, alleges ‘fraud’ in scanning process
content: In a separate social media post, Congress communication chief Jairam Ramesh alleged a major data breach in the CBSE’s Class 12 examination system, claiming that answer sheets of nearly two million students were available in the public domain.

title: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah resigns, but won’t budge from State politics
content: Going down memory lane, he said he was an ‘accidental politician’, with none in the family being in politics

title: D.K. Shivakumar to be sworn-in as Karnataka Chief Minister at Glass House in Lok Bhavan on June 3
content: KPCC Working President G.C. Chandrashekar appeals to party workers not to come to venue to reduce inconvenience to people

title: Shivakumar, Siddaramaiah to take Karnataka Cabinet formation talks to Delhi
content: D.K. Shivakumar and outgoing Chief Minister Siddaramaiah expected make their choices known to the Congress central leadership

title: White Paper on State govt.’s finances is being finalised
content: While assuming office last month, CM Vijay said his govt. would release the document, alleging that the DMK govt. left behind an empty treasury. It is likely to be out in a few days, says an official

title: Five killed, at least 10 injured in road accident in Chickballapur
content: The incident occurred when the victims were returning home in an autorickshaw after completing their day’s work. They were daily-wage labourers

title: ‘Nexbax AI Index’ to redefine AI adoption in India
content: It argues that the metrics dominating AI development in the world are essentially useless for the billion-plus users across India and the Global South

title: CUET-UG 2026 exam, rescheduled over Id, conducted smoothly across centres
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) conducted the rescheduled Common University Entrance Test-Undergraduate 2026 examination in two shifts at centres across the country and overseas

title: U.P.'s 'cockroach man' floats CJP spin-off after drawing attention to Yamuna's plight
content: The main reason behind dressing up as a cockroach was to tap the sudden interest centred on the insect to highlight neglect of the Yamuna river, among other things, says  Deepak Sharma

title: Corruption crackdown: Rajasthan government dismisses 20 officials, suspends 332
content: Among those dismissed are a Rajasthan Administrative Service (RAS) officer and several officials from departments including medical, education, mining and public works.

title: Sri Lankan Tamil girl killed after gang rams SUV into two-wheeler on Chennai’s Koyambedu flyover
content: A six-member gang first picked a fight with her and then assaulted her before driving the SUV into her two-wheeler, say the police

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Daughters killed for dowry
content: In the past three weeks, 33-year-old Twisha Sharma’s death over alleged dowry harassment has seen alleged procedural lapses and institutional bias by  Bhopal Police. As her mother-in-law, a retired judge, slandered her daughter-in-law, a former model on TV channels, Twisha’s parents and sibling stood up against the influential in-laws. An exhausting timeline of the case and the debate it has sparked about walking out of a bad marriage

title: NSA Ajit Doval meets Myanmar President Aung Hlaing
content: Hlaing set to meet PM Modi on Monday; issues relating to border security and connectivity to be discussed during the Myanmarese leader’s trip to India

title: SIR enumeration phase begins in Odisha, Mizoram, Sikkim and Manipur
content: Enumeration forms must be submitted by June 28 to be included in draft electoral rolls; this is part of the third phase of the SIR, which will complete the process across the country except Himachal, J&K, Ladakh

title: Granite quarries in north Kerala under increased surveillance after NIA raids
content: Move amid unconfirmed reports that legally procured explosives could be diverted for anti-social activities; inspections to ensure compliance with explosives-handling norms

title: Lok Adalats, DLSAs have no authority to grant divorce: Allahabad High Court
content: The court, in an order passed on April 30, said the power to dissolve a marriage rests exclusively with family courts, while the authority of Lok Adalats is limited to facilitating settlements between parties, and it cannot render judicial decisions.

title: Nepal has also encroached upon Indian territory, says PM Balendra Shah
content: Lawmakers from the Nepali Congress and the Nepal Communist Party demanded that the Prime Minister clarify where exactly Nepal has encroached upon Indian territory and sought the removal of his remarks from the record.

title: The persecution of consent
content: It is important for the law, courts, and the police to handle with a degree of sensitivity the question of consensual, underage relationships, simply because the tangle ensuing from this ‘offence’ has upended many young lives

title: Manoj Jarange Patil ends fast, demands separate Ministry for Maratha community
content: Maharashtra government representatives hand over a 12-point proposal to the Maratha reservation activist in connection with the reservation.

title: Sitting Vice-Chancellor on search panel raises conflict of interest concerns, says expert
content: The Department of Agriculture, through a notification dated May 14, constituted a search committee to recommend a panel of eligible candidates for the post of Vice-Chancellor of KSNUAHS, Shivamogga

title: 17-year-old from Bengaluru listed in Forbes 30 under 30
content: Soon after the launch, Divaa Uthkarsha’s Project Surya has reached over 4,02,000 individuals and directly supported 3,190 children living with Type 1 diabetes by raising over ₹20 lakh in funds

title: Gujarat CM participates in toy collection drive for underprivileged children
content: The campaign, inspired by Union Home Minister Amit Shah, aims to encourage children from well-off families to donate toys they no longer use to underprivileged children.

title: Bihar BJP’s Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya mega training campaign-2026 concludes
content: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary addressed the concluding session in Bhagalpur, while State President Sanjay Saraogi addressed training sessions in Motihari and Valmikinagar. Around 20,000 party workers across 52 organisational districts received Ideological and Organisational Training.

title: Overseas French nationals cast votes to elect councillor
content: Elected by direct universal suffrage for a six-year-term, the Councilors represent French citizens residing abroad and sit on the Consular Council

title: Padmaja assumes office as Collector of Kallakurichi
content: J.E. Padmaja said the administration would ensure expeditious redressal of grievances and strengthen inter-departmental coordination to drive the overall development of the district

title: As six die in Delhi building collapse, residents allege authorities turned a blind eye to illegal construction
content: The building collapsed on a canteen where doctors preparing for tests such as NEET-PG and FMGE were having food on Saturday evening; residents say illegal construction continued despite a notice issued against it, and that the MCD had even told the court that the construction had been stopped.

title: Stagnant sewage at bus terminus in Tiruvannamalai irks commuters
content: Commuters said that apart from travellers, buses also have to navigate through the stagnated sewage to reach the bus bays

title: College student dies after getting stuck in temple car wheel in Tiruchengode
content: When the temple car took a sharp left turn during procession, two young men who were closer to the back wheel of the temple car got stuck between the wheel and the wall of the building where the temple car is usually stationed.

title: Recruitment exam candidates accuse Uttar Pradesh govt of suppressing drive for transparency
content: The Hunkar Manch threatened indefinite protests and demanded that a technical monitoring system be implemented to ensure all future competitive examinations are completely transparent and secure

title: T.N. government announces zero-tolerance approach towards delays in sanctioning building plan approvals
content: State warns planning officials of disciplinary action for negligence and violation of transparency processes

title: BJP to host Nepal ruling party delegation in outreach to new govt.
content: The visit of the delegation assumes significance as it comes days after the cancellation of Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri’s proposed visit to Kathmandu

title: The apex court rings its own chain
content: The Supreme Court’s reliance on suo motu cognisance has turned a once ‘rare but highly visible’ jurisdiction into a recurring instrument shaped by primetime attention and media reports; while it keeps ‘ringing’ its own chain through televised listings and supervision, the trial courts below it continue doing the work

title: Commercial LPG Prices Hiked By Rs 42 In Delhi, Rs 53.5 In Kolkata Amid Iran war
content: In Delhi, a 19-kg commercial cylinder cost Rs 1,691.50 in January. With repeated revisions, the price has now climbed to Rs 3,113.50.

title: 39 Hardinge Road: The Reason Why Rabri Devi Doesn't Want To Vacate Patna Bungalow
content: Rabri Devi, 67, doesn't want her political career to go south; hence, she is trying to avoid the so-called unlucky house altogether.

title: Pakistan Spy Agency's New Plan Involves Making Terrorists Join Political Parties
content: The strategy is calculated: embed operatives within political structures to evade counter-terror surveillance and run terror networks under the veneer of democratic activity.

title: Arty Banana, Eaten Earlier By Hungry Tourist, Stolen From France Museum
content: The missing fruit -- which was taped to a wall to form the provocative work by Cattelan called "Comedian" -- was noticed by a guard on Saturday to have gone missing.

title: Manipur Police Flag Disinformation Against Security Forces And Cops, To Take Legal Action
content: Disinformation bid on social media comes at a time when Manipur's common people cutting across community lines are trying to return to their normal lives and live in peace, officials said

title: US-Iran Nuclear Deal Could Unlock $300 Billion For Tehran: Report
content: On the nuclear issue, Iran would agree not to develop nuclear weapons, while both sides would later decide the fate of Iran’s uranium stockpile and enrichment activities in a final deal

title: Argument In Chennai Bar Escalates Into Road Rage, Car Rams 2-Wheeler, Kills Woman
content: An altercation between two groups in a bar led to a road rage, killing an 18-year-old woman in Chennai

title: Police Case Against Ex-Bengal Sports Minister Over Messi Event Mess
content: The FIR alleges that Biswas, ahead of the Argentine footballer's event in Kolkata, had demanded a large number of tickets for his own distributions.

title: 'Companions In Happiness, Sorrow': Madhya Pradesh MLA Praises Chambal Dacoit
content: Dreaded dacoit Rambabu Gadariya, who was killed by the police in an encounter, was publicly remembered, garlanded and hailed as a "friend through happiness and sorrow" by the sitting BJP MLA, Pritam Lodhi.

title: Villagers Vandalise Trinamool MP's Party Office In Bengal's South 24 Parganas
content: A police officer said the situation was brought under control, and the assembled people were dispersed.

title: Google's 32 Million-Strong Mosquito Army To Fight...Mosquitoes
content: The technique of sterilising pest insects to control their numbers is not a new one and has been discussed as a chemical-free alternative for decades.

title: Vice President's 'Youngsters Will Follow The Cockroach' Warning To Media
content: C P Radhakrishnan said positive developments should receive greater attention in the media so that young people are exposed to the right information and role models.

title: Ladakh's New Excise Policy Raises Liquor Outlets From 2 To 20, Eases Rules
content: According to the Ladakh administration, it's for the first time that retail vending of liquor has been allowed in guest houses and homestays on payment of licence fees

title: Attack On Abhishek Banerjee In Bengal Gives INDIA Bloc Fresh Momentum
content: Abhishek Banerjee was allegedly set upon by locals and beaten up when he tried to visit the family of a party leader who allegedly died in poll violence.

title: Video: Huge Explosion Rocks Myanmar Building, Children Among 45 Dead
content: About 70 other people were injured in the explosion that took place around noon in the village of Kaungtup, in Namhkam township.

title: Food Regulator Seeks Explanation From Blinkit On Bad Quality Curd Complaint
content: The FSSAI has directed Blinkit to provide an explanation and also submit a comprehensive Action Taken Report (ATR).

title: Medieval Castle Captured In Israel's Deepest Push Into Lebanon In Decades
content: The capture of Beaufort castle came after days of airstrikes and intense fighting in nearby villages where Israeli troops fought Hezbollah members.

title: 'High command assured me': Siddaramaiah's son on cabinet berth prospects in DKS govt
content: the son of outgoing chief minister siddaramaiah says no final decision has been taken . the party high command has assured him of a ministerial berth . he says he met chief minister-designate D K Shivakumar to personally congratulate him .

title: Trump's messy makeover reveals golden age of chaos
content: the apparent contradiction has become a metaphor for a presidency increasingly marked by conflicting narratives, stalled diplomatic initiatives, domestic legal setbacks . analysts are reaching for aspirin as they track a white house that often resembles reality television in which the star keeps announcing victory while the sets collapse behind him .

title: IPL 2026: Bengaluru began victory lap way before RCB scored winning runs
content: Vindhya Pabolu is a correspondent covering a wide range of consumer-related, RERA, legal and civic issues. Her reports focus on everyday challenges faced by residents—ranging from water & garbage mismanagement to property disputes, traffic. Her stories are rooted in real voices and local contexts, with a focus on accountability and systemic gaps in Urban administration. Read More

title: Go back to people, find out how reforms have helped: PM Modi to minsters
content: PM Modi has directed all ministries and departments to go back to intended beneficiaries . ministerial colleagues and top bureaucrats had made presentations on reforms . toi says the reforms must focus on people and take into account every aspect .

title: Nepal, India encroaching on each other's land, UK must step in: PM Balen Shah
content: he said both India and Nepal had encroached on each other's territory . the border dispute had seen a flare-up early may - just over a month after Shah was sworn in . he urged the UK to take interest in addressing the issue .

title: RBI MPC may hike inflation forecast, trim growth rate
content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: Government braces for a tough sowing season ahead
content: the centre will launch a month-long nationwide campaign on balanced and judicious use of inputs such as fertilisers and water . the aim of the campaign is also to motivate farmers to increase the use of green manure, organic and bio products and opt for precision irrigation . agriculture minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan said awareness and practical interventions had become essential keeping in view rising temperatures, indiscriminate and imbalanced use of chemicals and pesticides .

title: BJP team meets at PM house to finalise RS, MLC poll picks
content: BJP's central election committee met at PM Narendra Modi's residence on Sunday to finalise the names of candidates . the meeting came a day before the filing of nomination for RS elections begins . a dispute between the state BJP brass and its two Maharashtra allies over their share of MLC seats might have also prompted top party functionaries .

title: CBSE crisis deepens: Student data exposed, say cyber activists
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: Mamata Banerjee calls off meet with TMC MLAs after only 20 of 80 turn up
content: Mamata banerjee called off a scheduled meeting with her party's legislators after only 20 of 80 MLAs turned up . the party blamed the mass absence on a "sudden and emergent ground situation" statewide attacks on senior functionaries and several arrests . "all our legislators have said they stand firmly with the party during these difficult times," she said .

title: PoK man caught in Uri, cops suspect affair
content: intruder identified as Zeeshan Ahmad Mir, 22, a resident of Painkadi village . he was caught late Saturday as he entered through the Haji Peer range in Uri’s Silikote area . local authorities later detained suspected to be the lady Mir wanted to meet .

title: Stuff of champions: Virat Kohli, ruthless bowling attack power RCB to consecutive IPL crowns
content: royal Challengers Bengaluru beat Gujarat Titans by five wickets in a one-sided final at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday night . 'King' Kohli smashed arshad Khan over long-on for six to finish things off with two overs to spare . the modern great brought up a 25-ball half-century, the fastest of his IPL career .

title: SC: Law is against commercialising prostitution, doesn't want to ban it
content: the Supreme Court has ruled that the principal objective of the legislation is not to abolish prostitution . it aims for is the inhibition or abolition of the commercialisation of prostitution, i.e., as an organised means of living .

title: ‘BJP murdering democracy’: Mamata after back-to-back assaults on Abhishek, Kalyan
content: former CM Mamata Banerjee accuses BJP of "murdering democracy" he says he was struck by a projectile, causing a bleeding head wound . she says a CRPF jawan saved his life .
